stem: What is the sum of the coefficients of the polynomial \( P(x) = ([[a]]x + [[b]])^{[[n]]} \)?
solution: The sum of the coefficients of \( P(x) \) equals \( P(1) = ([[a]] \cdot 1 + [[b]])^{[[n]]} = ([[a]] + [[b]])^{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This distributes the exponent over the sum; \( (a+b)^{n} \ne a^{n} + b^{n} \).
This multiplies by \( n \) instead of raising to the \( n \)th power.
This uses an exponent one too small.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.

stem: For \( P(x) = ([[a]]x + [[b]])^{[[n]]} \), what is the sum of the coefficients of the EVEN-power terms of \( P(x) \) (i.e. the coefficients of \( x^{0}, x^{2}, x^{4}, \ldots \))?
solution: Since \( P(1) \) sums ALL coefficients and \( P(-1) \) sums them with alternating sign, adding the two cancels the odd-power terms and doubles the even-power ones: the even-power sum is \( \dfrac{P(1) + P(-1)}{2} = \dfrac{([[a]]+[[b]])^{[[n]]} + ([[b]]-[[a]])^{[[n]]}}{2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( P(1) \), the sum of ALL coefficients, not just the even-power ones.
This uses the ODD-power combination formula (a difference, not a sum) instead of the even one.
This is \( P(-1) \) alone, the alternating sum, not the even-power sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by two from the correct sum.

solution: By the Binomial Theorem, \( \displaystyle\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}r^{k} = (1+r)^{n} \). Here that is \( (1+[[r]])^{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This raises only \( r \) to the power \( n \), ignoring the binomial coefficients and the \( 1 \) term entirely.
This multiplies \( n \) by \( 1+r \) instead of raising \( 1+r \) to the power \( n \).
This uses an exponent one too small.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by two from the correct sum.

stem: The polynomial \( P(x) = ([[a]]x + [[b]])^{[[n]]} \) has coefficients \( c_0, c_1, \ldots, c_{[[n]]} \) (from the constant term up to the \( x^{[[n]]} \) term). What is the alternating sum \( c_0 - c_1 + c_2 - \cdots \)?
solution: The alternating sum of the coefficients equals \( P(-1) = ([[a]](-1) + [[b]])^{[[n]]} = ([[b]] - [[a]])^{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( P(1) \), the ordinary (non-alternating) sum of the coefficients.
This has the subtraction reversed inside the parentheses before raising to the power \( n \).
This distributes the exponent over the difference; \( (b-a)^{n} \ne b^{n} - a^{n} \) in general.
This is off by one from the correct alternating sum.
This is off by one from the correct alternating sum.
This is off by two from the correct alternating sum.

solution: By Vandermonde's identity, \( \displaystyle\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}^{2} = \binom{2n}{n} \). Here that is \( \binom{2\cdot[[n]]}{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the sum of the UNSQUARED binomial coefficients, \( \sum \binom{n}{k} = 2^{n} \), not their squares.
This reports only the single largest (middle) binomial coefficient, not the sum of all squares.
This overshoots using \( 4^{n} \), which would only be right if every term equaled \( \binom{n}{k}^2 \approx \) the largest term.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by one from the correct sum.
This is off by two from the correct sum.
